macro_summary: Markets head into the July 4th holiday weekend with the Nasdaq-100 down roughly 6% in June and the semi sector hit by a sharp profit-taking rotation, even as underlying fundamentals remain intact. A softer-than-expected June jobs print (57K vs. ~112K consensus) has materially reduced near-term Fed hike risk, with futures pricing 79.5% probability of no move at the July 28-29 FOMC. Big-tech earnings season kicks off in earnest around July 28-31, setting up a dense catalyst window for the ideas below. The historic SK Hynix Nasdaq IPO on July 10 adds a rare macro signal for AI-infrastructure demand confirmation.
screening_note: 6 independent overnight screening runs analyzed; minimum threshold to qualify is 2 appearances. All 5 tickers cleared the bar, with 3 appearing in all 6 runs.
key_events: July 6 — Markets reopen after 3-day holiday weekend; seasonality historically bullish (Nasdaq avg +3.51% in July over last 10 years)
July 10 — SK Hynix (SKHY) Nasdaq IPO debut; largest ADR listing in recorded market history (~$29.4B raise)
July 24 — US-China tariff truce expiry; binary risk for AAPL China exposure
July 28 — MSFT Q4 FY2026 earnings after close; Azure CC growth guidance (39-40%) in focus
July 28-29 — FOMC meeting; no hike expected at 79.5% probability odds
July 30 — AAPL Q3 FY2026 earnings after close; first major print of Tim Cook successor era
August 26 — NVDA Q2 FY2027 earnings; $91.7B revenue consensus

ideas.name: SK Hynix Inc. (ADS)
ideas.thesis: SKHY appeared in all 6 overnight screens — the strongest consensus signal of the session. The July 10 Nasdaq IPO debut is the largest ADR listing in recorded market history (~$29.4B), and for the first time it grants Western institutional investors direct, liquid access to the world's dominant HBM/AI memory supplier. SK Hynix holds 58% global HBM market share with its order book sold out through 2027 and posted 198% YoY revenue growth and a 72% operating margin in Q1 2026 — exceeding even NVIDIA's 65% — yet the stock trades at a single-digit forward P/E versus US AI chip peers at 25-35x, a gap HSBC attributes almost entirely to the 'Korea Discount' now closing on listing day.
ideas.catalyst: Nasdaq IPO first trading day: July 10, 2026
ideas.edge: HSBC forecasts a ~20% valuation re-rating on U.S. listing (P/B rising from 2.8x to 3.4x); Micron historically traded at 35% premium to SK Hynix due solely to US-investor access gap; DDR5 margins potentially reaching 90%; Morgan Stanley forecasts DRAM +62% and NAND +75% in 2026
ideas.bull_case: U.S. listing closes the valuation gap to peer Micron (historically 35% premium on access alone) while a sold-out HBM4 order book and 72% operating margins deliver fundamental upside; options availability shortly post-listing makes defined-risk call spreads an attractive Day 1 vehicle.
ideas.bear_case: Korean-market shareholders may use the Nasdaq debut as a liquidity event to trim, creating early supply overhang; geopolitical US-Korea trade tensions or an AI capex spending pause could compress HBM pricing rapidly given high fixed-cost exposure.

ideas.name: Microsoft Corporation
ideas.thesis: MSFT appeared in all 6 overnight screens with unanimous high conviction. The stock sits near the low end of its post-Q3 range (~$387 vs. a $382-$466 band) despite an AI revenue run rate exceeding $37B annualized growing 123% YoY and Q4 revenue guidance of $87.9B (above the $87.61B consensus). The July 28 earnings print is a hard-dated catalyst just 4 weeks away, and the 56-analyst consensus 12-month target of $561 implies a 44% upside gap — an unusually wide spread for a mega-cap on the cusp of a beat-and-raise quarter.
ideas.catalyst: Q4 FY2026 earnings: confirmed July 28, 2026 after market close; Azure CC growth guidance (39-40%) is the key beat/miss metric
ideas.edge: Consensus analyst 12-month price target of $561 implies ~44% upside from ~$390; stock sold off ~5% on Q3 despite beats, creating an asymmetric reset; Q4 EPS consensus $4.24 vs. $4.06 last-quarter beat; 21.95x P/E is historically cheap for MSFT at this growth profile
ideas.bull_case: A Q4 Azure beat above 40% CC growth combined with continued Copilot enterprise monetization could re-rate the stock sharply toward the $420-$450 mid-range, rewarding a pre-earnings call spread (e.g., Aug $390/$420) with favorable risk/reward.
ideas.bear_case: Higher-for-longer rate environment compresses growth multiples; any Azure guidance miss or AI Copilot adoption data below expectations would extend the post-Q3 selloff, and the stock has already demonstrated willingness to fall on beats.

ideas.name: NVIDIA Corporation
ideas.thesis: NVDA appeared in all 6 overnight runs, though conviction was split (3 high, 3 medium), reflecting the further-dated earnings catalyst and elevated sector volatility. The stock is ~18% off its 52-week high of $236.54 near $194 technical support after a sector-wide profit-taking event (SOX -6.7% in one session) disconnected from fundamentals — FY2026 revenue grew 65% YoY and Q2 FY2027 is guided at $91B. The SKHY Nasdaq IPO on July 10 serves as a direct HBM demand confirmation catalyst for NVDA's Vera Rubin platform, where all three major HBM4 suppliers are now qualified and supply bottlenecks are easing.
ideas.catalyst: Q2 FY2027 earnings: August 26, 2026; near-term catalyst is SKHY IPO on July 10 as HBM demand signal; Vera Rubin HBM4 qualification confirmed June 5, 2026
ideas.edge: 61-analyst consensus target of $301.62 implies 55.8% upside from ~$194; P/E of 29.8x TTM is modest for 65% revenue growth; pullback driven by profit-taking not fundamental deterioration — SOX index dropped 6.7% in one session with no negative data releases
ideas.bull_case: Current $194 support level combined with a 55%+ analyst target gap, easing HBM supply constraints, and the SKHY IPO acting as an AI-demand confirmation event creates a strong swing-trade setup ahead of the August 26 earnings print.
ideas.bear_case: Elevated semi-sector volatility widens option spreads, reducing the efficiency of defined-risk plays; a macro hawkish surprise at the July 28-29 FOMC or signs of hyperscaler capex slowdown could push NVDA through $194 support before the August earnings floor.

ideas.name: Apple Inc.
ideas.thesis: AAPL appeared in 4 of 6 overnight screens with predominantly medium conviction. The confirmed July 30 earnings date is the primary catalyst, and the setup is compelling: management guided June-quarter revenue growth of 14-17% against a prior analyst consensus of only 9.5%, the Services segment provides a high-margin recurring revenue buffer, and a fresh $100B buyback is authorized. A defined-risk bull call spread expiring early August captures the catalyst cleanly while managing downside from the July 24 tariff truce expiry — a binary China-exposure risk that screens consistently flagged.
ideas.catalyst: Q3 FY2026 earnings: confirmed July 30, 2026 after market close; July 24 US-China tariff truce expiry is an intervening binary risk
ideas.edge: Guided revenue growth of 14-17% vs. prior consensus of 9.5% — a substantial undershoot by the Street; last earnings reaction was +3.2% despite an EPS miss, suggesting stock is well-supported; options-implied move likely moderate given stable Services mix, making call spread premium attractive
ideas.bull_case: A revenue beat driven by the 14-17% guidance range closes the CEO-transition discount and Services gross margin expansion above 47.5% triggers multiple re-rating toward the $317 post-earnings high; $100B buyback provides a price floor.
ideas.bear_case: July 24 tariff truce expiry reintroduces China hardware tariff risk, directly pressuring iPhone ASPs and gross margins; a repeat EPS miss (as in Q2) could weigh on the stock even on a revenue beat, limiting call spread upside.

ideas.name: Invesco QQQ Trust
ideas.thesis: QQQ appeared in 2 of 6 overnight screens — at the minimum threshold — but offers a diversified macro wrapper around the earnings-season and FOMC catalysts flagged across all other ideas. The June jobs print (57K vs. ~112K expected) materially reduces near-term hike risk, the Nasdaq-100 underperformed the S&P 500 by roughly 6% in June creating a catch-up setup, and July is historically the index's strongest month (avg +3.51% over 10 years, 100% positive rate). A defined-risk July bull call spread bridges the holiday reopen through the FOMC and into big-tech earnings season.
ideas.catalyst: Market reopen July 6 (holiday-weekend seasonality); FOMC July 28-29 (no hike at 79.5% probability); big-tech earnings window July 28-31
ideas.edge: Nasdaq-100 down ~6% in June vs. S&P 500 +20.37% YoY — mean-reversion setup; JPMorgan raised 2026 S&P target to 7,800 implying ~5% further upside; soft jobs data (57K vs. 110-115K expected) pushes next realistic hike to October at earliest
ideas.bull_case: Fading hike risk, bullish July seasonality, and a dense earnings-beat window (MSFT July 28, AAPL July 30) drive a Nasdaq-100 catch-up trade; a bull call spread with mid-to-late July expiry captures the setup with defined downside.
ideas.bear_case: Any hawkish Fed surprise at July 28-29 FOMC or a string of earnings misses from mega-cap tech names could accelerate the June momentum reversal; the semi-sector overhang (NVDA, AMD, Micron volatility) adds index drag risk given QQQ's large tech concentration.
